body:
### Why this matters

Azure App Service .NET runtimes are periodically deprecated when the upstream framework version ages out or has significant vulnerabilities. Apps can keep running for a while after a runtime is hidden from the portal, but future platform removal can create urgent deployment or availability risk.

### What this control checks

This control finds App Service apps using a deprecated .NET stack according to the runtime metadata collected from Azure.

### Operational note

Runtime upgrades should be released like application changes: retarget frameworks, update dependencies, test startup and hosting behavior, use deployment slots for production apps, and validate telemetry after the swap.
name: Ensure App Service .NET runtime is currently supported
description: App Service apps using deprecated .NET stacks
timedControlSummary: Azure App Service apps using unsupported .NET stacks should be moved to a currently supported runtime.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Late .NET runtime migrations can require framework retargeting, dependency upgrades, deployment changes, and production restart planning.
remediationDescription:
## Azure remediation

> **Service-wide runtime upgrade (recommended):** upgrade App Service .NET apps as a release train. This allows teams to test target frameworks, NuGet dependencies, hosting model changes, and CI/CD build images consistently.

### Azure Portal

1. Open the affected App Service app.
2. Go to **Settings > Configuration > General settings**.
3. Change the .NET runtime stack to a supported version, such as .NET 8 or the latest supported runtime approved by your engineering baseline.
4. Redeploy the compiled application and validate startup, health checks, and logs.

### Azure CLI

Check the current runtime:

```bash
az webapp config show \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <app-name> \
  --query '{linuxFxVersion:linuxFxVersion,netFrameworkVersion:netFrameworkVersion}'
```

List supported Linux runtimes:

```bash
az webapp list-runtimes --os linux | grep DOTNET
```

For Linux App Service apps, set the supported runtime stack:

```bash
az webapp config set \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <app-name> \
  --linux-fx-version "DOTNETCORE|8.0"
```

For Windows App Service apps, update the framework/runtime setting in **Configuration > General settings**, then redeploy the application built for the target framework.

### References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-language-dotnetcore

timedControlRules.summary: Azure App Service apps using unsupported .NET stacks should be moved to a currently supported runtime.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late .NET runtime migrations can require framework retargeting, dependency upgrades, deployment changes, and production restart planning.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set .NET to a supported App Service runtime, for example DOTNETCORE|8.0 or the latest approved supported runtime.
